title: salix stylosa var. elliptica
intro: Salix stylosa var. elliptica, also known as the Narrow-leaved Willow or Narrow-leaved Sallow, is a variety of the Salix stylosa tree, native to Australia, which belongs to the willow family (Salicaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Salix stylosa var. elliptica include its ability to reduce fever, soothe respiratory problems, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the leaves and stems, then steeping them in hot water or mixing them with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
